name: Pan-Seared Scallops, Corn, and Tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove tough muscle from side of each scallop if necessary and halve any large scallops.
2. Pat scallops dry with paper towels and in a bowl toss with salt and thyme.
3. In a large non-stick skillet cook bacon over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until crisp, then transfer to paper towels to drain.
4. Increase heat to moderately high and in drippings remaining in skillet sear scallops, stirring occasionally, until golden and cooked through. Remove scallops to a clean bowl with a slotted spoon.
5. In the same skillet, heat oil over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking, then sauté scallions, stirring occasionally, until they begin to brown (about 1 minute).
6. Add corn and tomatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, until corn begins to brown (about 3 minutes). Return scallops to the skillet and heat through.
7. Season with salt and pepper as needed.
8. Serve scallops sprinkled with bacon.
